idea-plugin.name: Claude Code Glyph
idea-plugin.description:
A terminal tool window purpose-built for Claude Code in IntelliJ-based IDEs — forked from TermGlyph.

It renders Claude Code cleanly (no overlapping text) via xterm.js 6 (Unicode 11) in the IDE's JCEF browser and adds Claude-Code-specific power features:
  • Profiles — start each session from a chosen settings.json with model, permission-mode, env and isolated config-dir overrides. Pick from the New-Session popup on the + button.
  • Live status & effects — a gradient beam, a colour-changing tab and a glass status chip reflect whether Claude is thinking, running a tool, or waiting for permission. The chip shows model and context-window usage live (via injected hooks & statusLine); both the chip contents and the effects are fully configurable in Settings (cost is opt-in).
  • Context & rate-limit awareness — a context-window fuel gauge in the chip, and proximity warnings as the context window or the 5-hour usage quota fills up (per-session cost is optional).
  • Clean rendering, Find (Cmd/Ctrl+F), copy/paste, native side-by-side split, tab icons/titles that follow the running process, and IDE-theme following.
Works wherever the Web Browser (JCEF) plugin is available (IntelliJ IDEA Ultimate and most professional IDEs).
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0 — CCGlyph (fork of TermGlyph).
• Profiles: launch Claude Code from a chosen settings.json with model/permission/env/config-dir overrides + a New-Session picker.
• Live status bridge: gradient beam + colour tab + status chip driven by hooks & statusLine (thinking / tool / waiting / idle), with live model and context-window %. The chip contents and the effects are configurable in Settings; per-session cost is opt-in.
• Context dashboard with rate-limit warnings.
• Clean Claude Code rendering (xterm.js Unicode 11), multi-tab + native side-by-side split, find, copy/paste, process-aware tab icons, IDE-theme following.
